evidence_quotes.quote: Gambling with loot boxes and in game currencies make games dangerous or unplayable.
in game timewalls that force you to come back after some hours.
Every other game is launched unfinished, full of bugs and locking the content behind paid DLCs.

paragraphs.text: Greetings, As a gamer for more than 2\3 of my life I am deeply worried about the trajectory of the video games industry and the way corporations exploit the lack of regulation: Gambling with loot boxes and in game currencies make games dangerous or unplayable. I was a victim and was exploited by my own psychology messing up my sleep and my life according the the in game timewalls that force you to come back after some hours. Every other game is launched unfinished, full of bugs and locking the content behind paid DLCs. And on top of it the new trend is making everything a live service and a few years later pulling the plug from games that we bought without a plan for offline playability. All these are blatant exploitation of the consumers and there must be a way to stop. Sincerely, A concerned citizen
